records.ety: [Gr. my^qos myth, fable, tale, talk, speech: cf. F. mythe.]

defs: A story of great but unknown age which originally embodied a belief regarding some fact or phenomenon of experience, and in which often the forces of nature and of the soul are personified; an ancient legend of a god, a hero, the origin of a race, etc.; a wonder story of prehistoric origin; a popular fable which is, or has been, received as historical.
A person or thing existing only in imagination, or whose actual existence is not verifiable.
quotes: As for Mrs. Primmins's bones, they had been myths these twenty years.
